description: Risktaking (also: risktakingness, risktakingity, risktakinging, trait) is a personality facet that captures the degree to which a conversational turn exhibits 'risktaking'. Context: Stable traits and dispositions: HEXACO/Big-Five facets, character strengths, virtues, risk-taking, integrity, authenticity. A high score on Risktaking indicates that the speaker's words, tone, or choices strongly reflect this quality; a low score indicates its absence or the presence of the opposing tendency.
positive_indicators: ["Clearly demonstrates risktaking in their response", "The turn contains strong evidence of risktaking", "The speaker's language consistently reflects risktaking"]
negative_indicators: ["Absence of any signal related to risktaking", "The turn contradicts or undermines risktaking", "No personality indicator for risktaking is present"]
score_1_anchor: Strong opposite tendency — behaviour is antithetical to risktaking.
score_2_anchor: Weak or incidental signal; risktaking is mostly absent.
score_3_anchor: Mixed — some evidence of risktaking but also contradictory signals.
score_4_anchor: Clearly present — turn shows consistent evidence of risktaking.
score_5_anchor: Strongly exemplifies risktaking; central, pronounced, unmistakable.
examples: ["A speaker says something that clearly reflects risktaking.", "The conversation turn is dominated by risktaking."]
